description_long: This is a large commercial jet airliner with a streamlined fuselage, swept-back wings, and a pair of underwing turbofan engines. The wings are long and slender, featuring upward-angled winglets at the tips for aerodynamic efficiency. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with a series of windows running along the length of the passenger cabin. The exterior is primarily white with a dark blue tail fin, and the surface appears smooth and glossy. The airplane is designed for passenger transport and is typical of modern wide-body jets seen at airports.
description: A modern commercial jet airliner with swept-back wings, underwing engines, and a blue tail fin. The fuselage is streamlined and primarily white, with a smooth, glossy finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide wingspan, nose cone, and both engines mounted under the wings.
description_view_1: Left side profile highlighting the elongated fuselage, cockpit windows, and the blue tail fin.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section, including the vertical and horizontal stabilizers, and the full wingspan with winglets.
description_view_3: Right side profile similar to the left, showing the smooth fuselage, engine placement, and tail design.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with long, swept-back wings and upward-angled winglets at the tips.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with two main gear units and a nose gear (not visibly extended).
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows at the front of the fuselage.
